title: Configure a small service
description: Model service configuration with owned fields, defaults, validation, and borrowed lookup APIs.

title: Parse a user from text
description: Build a parser around typed errors, Display, Error, sources, From, TryFrom, and behavior checks.

title: Summarize inventory
description: Use structs, borrowed collection outputs, folds, readable loops, IntoIterator, and borrowed sort comparisons.

title: Inspect log lines
description: Represent log entries and views with borrowing, lifetimes, pattern matching, Cow, and focused tests.

title: Build a request API
description: Design a small request builder with consuming setters, Default, Result, TryFrom, and doc examples.

title: Email address value object
description: Model, validate, format, and parse an email address with strong domain types and standard conversions.

title: Money and currency domain representation
description: Represent money and currency as a secure domain value object with invariants and checked operations.

title: Host and port configuration modeling
description: Configure hostnames and ports with strong typing, validation, defaults, and composition.

title: Converting DTOs into domain commands
description: Translate raw adapter data into validated domain commands and outbound DTOs with standard conversions.

title: Exposing iteration on collection wrappers
description: Wrap collections while exposing idiomatic iteration and fallible construction APIs.

title: Hierarchical configuration loader errors
description: Design configuration loading errors with Display, Error, source preservation, From, and typed propagation.

title: Translating errors across boundaries
description: Map domain and repository failures into application and adapter-safe error responses.

title: Register user use case boundaries
description: Keep domain, application ports, infrastructure implementations, DTO adapters, and handler boundaries separate.

title: Structured request logging
description: Represent logs as structured events with levels, request fields, spans, error kinds, and redaction.

title: Table-driven domain tests
description: Use table-driven tests, executable docs, and properties to protect bounded domain values used by pricing and rollout policy.
